titleStmt.title: The Minister in the Netherlands ( (Gordon) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Minister in the Netherlands ( Gordon ) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
The Hague , February 24, 1938—5 p.m.
[Received February 24—2:35 p.m.]

div.p: 20. Department’s 17, February 23, 7 p.m. Have conveyed message to Foreign Minister who has no objection to consultation. I had not requested text of proposed revision for transmission to Department as I assumed it had been forwarded from London. I confirmed this by telephone conversation with London Embassy from which I further understood that VilesA. L. Viles, president of the Rubber Manufacturers Association, and American representative on the Consumers Panel of the International Rubber Regulation Committee. has fully informed the Department as to the subcommittee’s deliberations. I take it therefore in the absence of further instructions that after examination of the text of the proposed revision the Department will send me further specific views for presentation to the Foreign Office which will initiate the consultation in question.
